content:
A simple client conversation reminded me why programming fundamentals still matter in the age of AI.

3 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Gabriel Heinzer on UnsplashRecently, someone came to meet me about developing a VTU application for him. It was going to be a small finance application for selling data bundles, cable subscriptions, airtime, and bill payments.
At the time, I was at my place of work. As a classroom teacher, I had to be in school, but I made out some time so we could sit down and discuss the project.
In the middle of our conversation, he told me something a friend of his had said.
His friend believed that, considering the era of AI, a VTU platform could now be built very easily.
But according to him, once the application breaks down, that’s the end of it.
He said it would become difficult to fix, or even impossible to regain control of the application.
I smiled.
Then I told him something.
“No.”
“The application is only ‘lost’ to someone who relies entirely on AI without understanding software development.”
